_meta.description: Character kits — optional specializations that customize a base class with additional proficiencies, equipment, role-playing restrictions, and special abilities.

kit_rules.description: A kit is chosen at character creation and cannot be changed later. Kits provide bonuses and impose restrictions that define the character's background and role.
kit_rules.selection: Players choose one kit (or no kit) at character creation.
kit_rules.requirements: Each kit has specific ability score, race, alignment, or class requirements that must be met.
kit_rules.benefits: Bonus proficiencies, reduced-cost proficiencies, special abilities, or social advantages.
kit_rules.hindrances: Required behavior, restricted gear, social stigmas, or mechanical penalties that reinforce the character concept.

fighting_styles.description: Fighters (and warriors) may specialize in a fighting style by spending one weapon proficiency slot. Each style grants specific bonuses.

styles.name: Single Weapon Style
benefits: AC improves by 1 when fighting without a shield or off-hand weapon
Attackers suffer -1 penalty attacking the fighter
styles.notes: Best for dueling or when a shield is unavailable

styles.name: Weapon & Shield Style
benefits: +1 to shield's AC bonus (shield provides AC -2 instead of -1)
Attacker's critical-hit threshold increases by 1 (optional rule)

styles.name: Two-Weapon Style
benefits: Reduces primary hand attack penalty from -2 to 0
Reduces off-hand attack penalty from -4 to -2
styles.notes: Stacks with Ranger's natural two-weapon ability. Off-hand weapon must be smaller or equal size to primary.

styles.name: Two-Handed Weapon Style
benefits: +2 to damage rolls when wielding a two-handed melee weapon
Initiative tie goes in the fighter's favor
styles.notes: Does not apply to two-handed bows or crossbows

styles.name: Thrown Weapon Style
benefits: +1 to attack rolls with thrown weapons
Can draw and throw a weapon as part of the same action without penalty
styles.notes: Halflings with this style gain an additional +1 (stacks with racial bonus)

styles.name: Archery Style
benefits: Can fire into melee without the standard penalty (targets are still randomized on a miss)

weapon_groups.description: An optional system grouping related weapons. Proficiency in one weapon grants limited ability with related weapons in the same group.
weapon_groups.cross_group_use: A character proficient in one weapon from a group uses other weapons in that group at -2 to hit (instead of full non-proficiency penalty).

kits.name: Amazon
kits.description: A female warrior from a society of warrior women, skilled in survival and independent fighting.
kits.armor_allowed: Leather, studded leather, chain mail — no plate or splint
special_benefits: Can fight from horseback at no penalty even without mounted combat proficiency
+2 to saves vs. charm and domination
special_hindrances: Must refuse aid from men in personal combat (will accept tactical cooperation)
Social disadvantage in patriarchal societies (-2 reaction from domineering male NPCs)
kits.notes: Amazon fighters work best in settings with matriarchal or tribal cultures.

kits.name: Barbarian
kits.description: A warrior from a primitive or uncivilized culture, relying on raw power and battlefield instinct rather than formal training.

requirements.INT: max 10 at start (can rise with experience)

kits.armor_allowed: Leather, studded leather, ring mail — distrusts heavier armor
special_benefits: Rage: once per day, enter a battle rage granting +2 to hit, +4 to damage, +5 HP (temporary), and immunity to fear for 5 rounds; afterward fatigued for 5 rounds (-2 to hit and damage)
Alertness: surprised only on a 1 on 1d10
+2 to save vs. spells while in wilderness
special_hindrances: Cannot read or write at character creation (can learn with proficiency slot)
Distrusts magic: -2 to saves vs. beneficial spells if cast on them without consent
Will not wear metal armor voluntarily
Cannot multiclass
kits.notes: Barbarian fighters are powerful melee combatants but face social and magical disadvantages.

kits.name: Berserker
kits.description: A warrior who channels savage fury in combat, fighting without regard for personal safety.
special_benefits: Berserker rage: +2 to attack rolls and damage; immune to fear and morale effects; continues fighting at 0 HP (until -10 HP)
Entering rage: automatic when reaching 0 HP; voluntary before combat on a WIS check (failure = must enter rage)
Intimidation: enemies with fewer HD must save vs. wand or suffer -1 to attack rolls
special_hindrances: Must attack nearest creature (friend or foe) when in uncontrolled rage unless WIS check made each round
After rage: collapses unconscious for 1d6 turns
Charisma -2 vs. non-barbarian NPCs in civilized areas
kits.notes: Berserkers are unpredictable in combat but devastating when controlled.

kits.name: Cavalier
kits.description: A noble warrior trained in mounted combat and chivalric traditions.
special_benefits: +2 to attack rolls while mounted
+2 to reaction rolls from nobles and aristocrats
Warhorse provided at character creation (free)
Can spend 1 proficiency slot to learn a new weapon for half normal cost (noble training)
special_hindrances: Must adhere to a code of chivalric conduct or lose benefits
Cannot dismount to fight if mount is still functional
Must challenge enemies of equal or greater rank to single combat (or face CHA penalty)
Minimum 10% of income must be donated to liege or temple
kits.notes: Cavaliers function best in organized, feudal settings.

kits.name: Gladiator
kits.description: A warrior trained in arena combat, skilled at putting on a show while defeating opponents.
kits.armor_allowed: Any (but prefers light armor for showmanship)
special_benefits: +1 to hit and damage vs. opponents of same or lower HD after studying them for 2 rounds
May disarm opponents on any roll 4+ above minimum needed to hit
Spectators: if performing for a crowd of 20+ people, gains +1 morale/attack from their cheering
Can earn gold through arena combat (1d10 x 10 gp per fight won in a major arena)
special_hindrances: Must fight in a crowd-pleasing style (no missile weapons in major fights; dramatic finishes)
Subject to arena law and owners if still under contract
Recognized publicly: may be challenged to fights by fans or rivals
kits.notes: Gladiators excel in one-on-one combat and have strong CHA-based social abilities.

kits.name: Landsknecht
kits.description: A mercenary soldier trained in group tactics, large weapons, and the economics of war.
special_benefits: +1 to morale checks for nearby allies (within 20 ft)
Knows standard mercenary rates and can negotiate fair pay
Can lead a mercenary company as a henchman group at lower loyalty cost
special_hindrances: Must be paid regularly or morale drops
Alignment shifts toward money: may refuse missions without adequate pay
Cannot serve the same employer for more than 1 year without renegotiating
kits.notes: Landsknechts are professional soldiers — reliable but mercenary in outlook.

kits.name: Myrmidon
kits.description: An elite soldier devoted entirely to military perfection, with no outside interests or social distractions.
special_benefits: +1 to all attack rolls due to pure military focus
Immune to morale failure
Can maintain proficiency with a weapon group with fewer practice slots
special_hindrances: Social awkwardness: -2 to CHA-based rolls outside military/combat context
Cannot own more possessions than can be carried
Must follow orders from superior officers absolutely
kits.notes: Myrmidons are supreme combat specialists but poor social characters.

kits.name: Noble Warrior
kits.description: A fighter born into the nobility who combines martial training with courtly education.
special_benefits: +2 to reaction rolls in social situations with nobles and royalty
Free access to a keep or manor (modest) as inherited property
Starts with a personal seal and letters of introduction
special_hindrances: Must maintain standards of nobility (clothing, behavior, entourage)
Obligations to liege lord and family
Cannot perform menial labor openly (servants must do it)
kits.notes: Noble Warriors are strong social characters with built-in campaign hooks.

kits.name: Peasant Hero
kits.description: A common-born fighter who rose to heroism through sheer determination and practical experience.
kits.armor_allowed: Any, but starts with only leather or padded
special_benefits: +2 to reaction rolls among common people and peasants
Doubled XP for defeating enemies specifically threatening common folk
Can improvise weapons from farm tools with no nonproficiency penalty
special_hindrances: -2 to reaction rolls from nobles and upper class
Lacks formal military training: cannot use cavalry tactics or cavalry formations
Starts with minimal equipment (see wealth)
kits.notes: Peasant Heroes connect strongly to commoner NPCs and grassroots storylines.

kits.name: Pirate/Buccaneer
kits.description: A seafaring fighter skilled in shipboard combat, plunder, and the lawless life at sea.
kits.armor_allowed: Leather, studded leather only (heavier armor risks drowning)
special_benefits: +2 to attack rolls when fighting aboard ships
Can climb ship rigging at full movement speed
Can intimidate ship crews for surrender without combat (CHA check)
special_hindrances: Wanted by law in most civilized nations
Distrusts landlocked environments: -1 to all rolls when more than 30 miles from the sea
Must share plunder with crew
kits.notes: Pirates function best in nautical or coastal campaigns.

kits.name: Samurai
kits.description: A disciplined warrior from an Eastern culture who follows a strict code of honor.
special_benefits: +1 to attack rolls with katana or wakizashi (Eastern swords)
Once per day: Iaijutsu — a lightning-fast first strike that acts at initiative 1 and deals double damage if it hits
+2 to saves vs. fear and morale effects
Can commit seppuku to die with honor
special_hindrances: Must follow Bushido — code of honor, loyalty to lord, and ritual conduct
Cannot use ranged weapons except in siege or naval contexts
If lord dies or dishonored, becomes Ronin (loses CHA bonus; gains freedom)
kits.notes: Best used in Eastern-themed campaigns (Kara-Tur setting).

kits.name: Scout
kits.description: A light-armored fighter trained in reconnaissance, quick movement, and woodcraft.
kits.armor_allowed: Leather, studded leather, ring mail only
special_benefits: Surprised only on a 1 on 1d10
Can move at full speed while tracking (no -2 penalty for moving quickly)
Moves silently outdoors on a 4-in-6
special_hindrances: Must travel light: maximum encumbrance = Light burden
Reluctant to engage in pitched battle — prefers hit-and-run
Will not wear heavy armor
kits.notes: Scouts are excellent for wilderness and dungeon exploration roles.

kits.name: Swashbuckler
kits.description: A flashy, acrobatic fighter who relies on speed, style, and wit more than brute strength.
special_benefits: AC bonus: DEX defensive bonus applies even in melee when wearing no armor or leather
May use DEX modifier for attack rolls instead of STR when using light melee weapons (short sword, dagger, main gauche)
+2 to reaction rolls in social encounters due to charm and flair
May attempt flashy disarms and taunts (CHA check) to distract opponents (-2 to their next attack)
special_hindrances: Will not wear heavy armor under any circumstances
Must maintain stylish appearance — refuses to look disheveled
Overconfident: DM may force a WIS check before engaging clearly superior opponents
kits.notes: Swashbucklers are the 'rogue-ish fighter' archetype — great for urban and nautical settings.

kits.name: Wilderness Warrior
kits.description: A fighter who specializes in wilderness survival and combat against natural and monstrous threats.
kits.armor_allowed: Any up to chain mail (no plate)
special_benefits: +1 to attack rolls vs. one chosen monster type (chosen at creation, same as ranger favored enemy)
Can identify animal tracks automatically within 30 ft
No penalty for fighting in difficult terrain (swamps, forests, mountains)
special_hindrances: Uncomfortable in cities: -1 to attack rolls and saves in urban environments
Cannot have a permanent home or stronghold until 9th level
Must spend at least 1 week per month in the wilderness or suffer -1 to rolls
kits.notes: A good middle ground between Fighter and Ranger roles.

kits.name: Acrobat
kits.description: A thief trained in acrobatics, tumbling, and feats of physical daring.
special_benefits: +20% to Climb Walls
+10% to Move Silently
Reduce falling damage: 1d3 per 10 ft instead of 1d6 (Tumbling proficiency check)
Can cartwheel through a space occupied by an enemy without provoking an attack
special_hindrances: Must perform publicly to maintain reputation (or suffer -1 CHA per month of inactivity)
Overconfident about physical feats: must succeed a WIS check to decline a physical challenge

kits.name: Adventurer
kits.description: A generalist thief who dabbles in dungeoncraft and adventuring rather than a specific criminal trade.
special_benefits: +5% to any one thief skill (chosen at creation)
Jack-of-all-trades: may use any normal equipment without penalty
special_hindrances: No specific specialty: never gains the higher bonuses of focused kits

kits.name: Assassin
kits.description: A killer for hire trained to eliminate targets efficiently and disappear without a trace.
special_benefits: Backstab: +2 to multiplier (x4 at level 1, instead of x2)
Disguise bonus: +10% to Disguise ability
Access to poisons through the assassin's guild at market prices
Can attempt to slay target in their sleep (no attack roll needed; target must save vs. death or die)
special_hindrances: Member of an assassin's guild — owes 20% of earnings
If alignment becomes good, kit is lost
Must take assassination contracts occasionally or lose guild standing

kits.name: Beggar-Thief
kits.description: A thief who uses poverty and pity as tools, extracting information and valuables through apparent helplessness.
special_benefits: +15% to Pick Pockets when in beggar's disguise
Learns rumors and news automatically (1d4 useful items per week in a settlement)
Treated as non-threatening — enemies with 5+ HD rarely attack first
special_hindrances: Stigmatized appearance: -3 reaction from upper class
Possessions limited to what can be concealed on the body
Depends on goodwill of lower-class NPCs

kits.name: Bounty Hunter
kits.description: A thief who specializes in tracking and capturing — or eliminating — fugitives.
special_benefits: Can legally hold captured criminals in most jurisdictions
+5% to Detect Noise
+10% to Move Silently while actively tracking a target
Advantage on opposed checks to prevent escape from grapples
special_hindrances: Cannot kill bounty targets (or loses full payment)
Must work within legal frameworks (or become wanted themselves)
Time pressure: bounties expire

kits.name: Burglar
kits.description: The archetypal thief — skilled at breaking and entering, cracking safes, and leaving without a trace.
special_benefits: +10% to Open Locks
+10% to Find/Remove Traps
+5% to Move Silently
Can assess a building's security layout by observing for 1 turn (INT check for accurate read)
special_hindrances: Known to thieves' guilds — must pay dues or be considered a freelancer (and targeted)
Cannot resist a challenge presented by a well-secured location

kits.name: Cutpurse
kits.description: A street thief specializing in quick theft of small valuables in crowds.
bonus_proficiencies: Crowd Reading (special — detects wealth on targets)
special_benefits: +15% to Pick Pockets
Can Pick Pockets in combat at -10% (instead of -25%)
Blend into crowds automatically (effectively invisible to casual observation)
special_hindrances: Cannot resist targets carrying obvious wealth
City-dweller: -2 to all rolls in wilderness
Little combat training: can only wear leather and use small weapons

kits.name: Investigator
kits.description: A thief whose skills are turned toward solving mysteries and gathering intelligence.
special_benefits: +10% to Detect Noise
+10% to Read Languages
Forensic observation: can read a crime scene for clues (INT check; DM provides useful detail)
+2 to reaction rolls from law enforcement NPCs
special_hindrances: Compelled to investigate mysteries — must WIS check to let a puzzle go unsolved
Reputation as a spy or informant in criminal circles

kits.name: Rake
kits.description: A gentleman or gentlewoman thief who operates among the upper class, stealing through charm and access rather than stealth.
special_benefits: +2 reaction from upper-class NPCs
+15% to Pick Pockets among nobles
May maintain a social cover identity as a noble or wealthy merchant at no cost
Immune to suspicion in high society unless caught in the act
special_hindrances: Must maintain an expensive lifestyle (minimum 20 gp/month)
Useless in lower-class or wilderness situations (-1 to most thief skills outside of social settings)
Cannot backstab a social ally

kits.name: Scout
kits.description: A thief trained in wilderness reconnaissance and intelligence gathering.
special_benefits: +10% to Move Silently outdoors
+10% to Hide in Shadows outdoors
Can read terrain and estimate enemy numbers (INT or WIS check)
Surprised only on a 1 on 1d10 in wilderness
special_hindrances: Cannot operate effectively in urban environments (-10% to all urban thief skills)
Reluctant to follow orders: independent streak

kits.name: Spy
kits.description: A professional intelligence gatherer who infiltrates organizations and extracts secrets.
special_benefits: +20% to Disguise ability
Cover identity: starts with one fully-documented false identity
Signal recognition: can pass and identify coded messages automatically
+10% to Pick Pockets while in disguise
special_hindrances: Works for an organization — must report and obey missions
If cover is blown, the organization may abandon or eliminate the spy
Can never fully trust anyone (WIS check to accept help without suspicion)

kits.name: Swashbuckler
kits.description: A dashing, bold thief who relies on speed and panache rather than skulking.
special_benefits: Use DEX for attack rolls with light weapons (short sword, dagger, rapier)
+2 to reaction rolls when acting dramatically or heroically
Surprise opponents on a 4-in-6 when charging from above or swinging on a rope
special_hindrances: Will not use poison — too dishonorable
Cannot refuse a challenge to fair combat
Must look stylish: minimum appearance standards

kits.name: Thug
kits.description: A street-level criminal who relies on intimidation, ambush, and brute force.
special_benefits: +2 to damage rolls in ambushes (prepared attacks)
Intimidate opponents with fewer HD at -2 attack rolls
+5% to backstab (in addition to normal multiplier)
special_hindrances: Crude: -2 reaction rolls in polite company
Criminal record in at least one city
Must belong to a criminal organization

kits.name: Academician
kits.description: A scholar-wizard whose magic is rooted in deep research and institutional learning.
special_benefits: +5% to Learn Spell rolls
+1 spell per level added to starting spellbook (bonus spells from research)
special_hindrances: Must maintain a library (minimum 500 gp value)
Reluctant to adventure unless research goals are served
Disdains illiterate companions (-1 reaction to non-readers)

kits.name: Amazon Sorceress
kits.description: A female warrior-mage from a society where magic and martial skill are equally prized.
special_benefits: May wear leather armor without arcane spell failure
Can use short sword and spear without nonproficiency penalty
+1 to attack rolls with short sword
special_hindrances: Must refuse magical aid from male spellcasters (personal code)
Social stigma in patriarchal societies

kits.name: Anagakok
kits.description: A shaman-mage from a primitive culture whose magic is tied to nature spirits and tribal tradition.
special_benefits: Can communicate with nature spirits (as Speak with Animals, 1x/day)
+2 to saves vs. natural magical effects (cold, lightning, fire from natural sources)
Totem animal: gains a spirit animal familiar granting specific abilities (DM choice based on culture)
special_hindrances: Cannot use manufactured scrolls or spellbooks — must carve spells into bone/stone tablets
Magic is tied to home terrain: -1 to spell effects when far from home environment
Must perform weekly ritual or lose 1 prepared spell slot

kits.name: Barbarian Mage
kits.description: A wizard who comes from a primitive culture, whose magic is instinctive and wild.
special_benefits: Can cast spells while wearing leather armor (no failure chance)
+2 CON modifier to HP per level (treating class as Fighter for CON bonuses)
Wild surge: once per day, may add +2 to a spell's caster level (but rolls on a small chaos table)
special_hindrances: Cannot read or write at character creation (buy proficiency to learn)
Will not use spellbook — memorizes spells through repetition (must review each spell for 2x longer)
Charisma penalty in civilized settings: -1 reaction

kits.name: Militant Wizard
kits.description: A wizard who has received formal military training, comfortable in martial environments.
special_benefits: Can use crossbows and daggers without nonproficiency penalty
+1 to initiative (military training)
Can wear leather armor without arcane spell failure
special_hindrances: Must maintain military discipline: cannot retreat from an ordered position without a CON or WIS check
Under obligation to a military organization

kits.name: Mystic
kits.description: A wizard who draws magic from meditation and spiritual discipline rather than academic study.
special_benefits: +1 to saves vs. all mind-affecting spells
Meditative trance: can recover 1 spell per hour of meditation (instead of full 8-hour rest) once per day
Inner calm: immune to magical fear 1x/day
special_hindrances: Must meditate 2 hours per day or suffer -1 to all spell rolls that day
Cannot use divination spells for personal gain without WIS check
Minimal material possessions (maximum 10 non-magical items)

kits.name: Patrician
kits.description: A wizard from a wealthy noble family who uses magic as a social and political tool.
special_benefits: +2 reaction rolls from upper-class and noble NPCs
Access to fine libraries: starts with 2 additional spells in spellbook
Wealthy patron: begins play with 100 gp monthly stipend (from family) — at DM's discretion
special_hindrances: Expensive tastes: minimum monthly expenses of 30 gp
Family obligations and political maneuvering
Cannot refuse a reasonable request from family patriarch/matriarch

kits.name: Peasant Wizard
kits.description: A hedge wizard from common stock who learned magic through folk tradition and observation.
special_benefits: +2 reaction from common folk and rural NPCs
Folk remedies: can brew minor medicinal cures (not magical — restores 1 HP after 8 hours rest without bed rest)
Earthy magic: +1 to Learn Spell for Alteration spells
special_hindrances: -2 reaction from upper-class NPCs who look down on common-born wizards
Limited access to libraries and high-level mentors
Starts with only 2 1st-level spells instead of the usual 4

kits.name: Savage Wizard
kits.description: A wizard from a tribal culture who learned magic from observation of the natural world.
special_benefits: Can memorize spells from nature (sky, fire, stone) as well as spellbook — 1 spell per day from meditation near the element
+1 to spells that mimic natural forces (lightning, fire, cold, wind)
Immune to non-magical heat and cold
special_hindrances: Cannot use city libraries or purchased scrolls to learn new spells
Spellbook is carved symbols on hide/stone — fragile and unusual
Literacy: cannot read standard script without proficiency

kits.name: Witch
kits.description: A practitioner of an ancient, nature-rooted magical tradition, often feared or revered by commoners.
special_benefits: Hedge spells: 3 minor cantrip effects per day usable without spell slots (minor light, minor chill, minor spark)
+2 reaction from common folk in rural areas
Familiar: automatically receives a cat, toad, or raven familiar (as Find Familiar) at 1st level at no cost
Can brew minor potions (healing, sleep, minor attribute boost) with time and materials
special_hindrances: Feared by superstitious populations: -2 reaction from organized religious communities
Cannot join a wizard's guild or formal magical institution
Must maintain a home (cottage, cave, or clearing) as a base for magical work

priest.notes: Priest kits in the Complete Priest's Handbook are typically organized around deity archetypes rather than named kits. The following represent the major specialty priest templates. See also adnd2e_classes_additional.json for the full specialty priest sphere system.

kits.name: Crusader
kits.description: A militant priest devoted to spreading the faith through battle.
kits.weapons_allowed: Any — crusaders are one of few priest types with unrestricted weapon use
special_benefits: +1 to attack rolls; clerical ability to turn undead; combat spells at one level lower casting cost (optional)
special_hindrances: Must convert non-believers by word or deed; no retreating from battle against the faith's enemies

kits.name: Healer
kits.description: A priest devoted entirely to the alleviation of suffering and restoration of health.
special_benefits: All healing spells cast at +1 HP per die
Can cast Cure Light Wounds once per day as a free action (not using a spell slot)
Immune to magical disease
special_hindrances: Cannot attack living creatures unless in self-defense
Must offer healing to any creature that requests it (can charge, but not refuse)
10% tithe on all earnings to maintain temple stores of medicine

kits.name: Diviner Priest
kits.description: A priest whose deity reveals truth and prophecy through their servant.
special_benefits: +2 to all Divination spell saves (others get -2 to resist)
Commune spell usable twice per week instead of once
Always knows True North and approximate time
special_hindrances: Must speak the truth — cannot lie even by omission under penalty of losing spells for 1 week
Compelled to share prophecies and information freely

kits.name: Nature Priest
kits.description: A priest serving a deity of the natural world, forests, or animals.
kits.weapons_allowed: Club, sickle, spear, sling, staff, dagger
special_benefits: Speak with Animals at will (as innate ability)
Move through natural terrain at normal speed (no difficult terrain penalty)
+2 to saves vs. natural environmental effects
special_hindrances: Cannot enter cities with populations over 5,000 without suffering -1 to all rolls per day
Will not damage wilderness areas — cannot start fires in forests

kits.name: Undead Hunter
kits.description: A priest whose divine mission is the destruction of undead.
special_benefits: Turn undead as a cleric 2 levels higher
+2 to all attacks vs. undead
Holy touch: once per day, cause maximum damage on one hit vs. undead
special_hindrances: Must pursue and destroy undead whenever encountered — cannot retreat from undead unless clearly outmatched
Cannot associate with necromancers or those who create undead

kits.name: Beastmaster
kits.description: A ranger who has bonded deeply with animals, able to befriend and command a diverse menagerie.
bonus_proficiencies: Animal Training (bonus — up to 3 animal types)
special_benefits: Can have twice the normal number of animal followers
+2 to tame wild animals (Animal Handling checks)
Animals of any type will not attack the Beastmaster unprovoked
special_hindrances: Must protect all animals in their care; cannot allow followers to be harmed deliberately
Must spend at least 1 hour per day with animal companions or they begin to wander
kits.weapon_restrictions: Swords, spears, bows, clubs only — no axes or manufactured war weapons

kits.name: Explorer
kits.description: A ranger devoted to discovering and mapping unknown territory.
special_benefits: +2 to all Navigation checks
Can accurately map explored territory from memory (INT check)
First in any area: surprise opponents on 4-in-6
special_hindrances: Compelled to explore unmarked territory — will take detours for new discoveries
Cannot use the same travel route twice if another is available

kits.name: Forest Runner
kits.description: A ranger deeply attuned to woodland environments.
special_benefits: Cannot be surprised in a forest
+2 to hit and damage vs. creatures that live in forests (undead, constructs excluded)
Track creatures in forest at +4 to skill
special_hindrances: Uncomfortable in non-forest terrain: -1 to all rolls in cities, deserts, or tundra
Will not use fire in a forest

kits.name: Giant Killer
kits.description: A ranger who specializes in fighting giant-sized creatures (giants, trolls, ogres, etc.).
special_benefits: +2 to hit and damage vs. any giant-type creature
+2 to saving throws vs. giant attacks
Never surprised by giant-type creatures
special_hindrances: Must confront giant-type creatures when encountered rather than evading them
Social reputation as a giant killer: constantly tested or challenged

kits.name: Justifier
kits.description: A ranger who serves as a lawman and enforcer of wilderness justice.
special_benefits: +2 to reaction from law-abiding settlers and villages
Can enforce arrest on criminals in the wilderness (legal authority recognized)
+2 to saves vs. illusion and deception magic
special_hindrances: Cannot allow criminals to escape unpunished if in a position to stop them
Must follow the letter of the law even in unjust situations — or lose authority

kits.name: Mountain Man
kits.description: A ranger adapted to life in extreme mountain and highland terrain.
special_benefits: Immune to altitude sickness
No movement penalty in rocky or mountain terrain
+2 to attacks and damage vs. creatures native to mountains (giants, wyverns, etc.)
special_hindrances: Uncomfortable at sea level: -1 to all rolls in coastal or swamp terrain
Prefers solitude: -1 to CHA-based rolls in groups larger than 5

kits.name: Stalker
kits.description: A ranger trained as an assassin of the wilderness — tracking and eliminating specific threats.
special_benefits: +20% to Move Silently
+10% to Hide in Shadows
+2 backstab multiplier when attacking from hiding (acts as partial backstab)
special_hindrances: Will not kill non-combatant animals or civilians under any circumstances
Must take a specific target for each major mission (no random violence)

kits.name: Wanderer
kits.description: A ranger with no fixed home, drifting between regions and cultures.
special_benefits: Can survive indefinitely in any terrain
+1 to reaction from all rural and wilderness NPCs regardless of culture
Immune to being lost — always knows approximate direction and distance to nearest settlement
special_hindrances: Cannot own property or establish a permanent home
Must move on from any settlement after 1 month maximum (or suffers -1 to all rolls from restlessness)

kits.name: Chevalier
kits.description: The quintessential mounted knight — all chivalric ideals embodied.
special_benefits: +2 to all mounted combat
Warhorse is always at max hit points
Free lance proficiency
special_hindrances: Must challenge all enemies to honorable combat before attacking — ambushing is forbidden
Must announce themselves before combat

kits.name: Errant
kits.description: A wandering knight seeking glory and trials of faith.
special_benefits: XP bonus 10% for completing quests involving discovery or exploration
+1 to random encounter reaction rolls (God looks after wanderers)
special_hindrances: Cannot refuse a quest bestowed by a superior or an innocent in need
No fixed home or stronghold allowed before 9th level

kits.name: Ghosthunter
kits.description: A paladin devoted to fighting undead and spirits specifically.
special_benefits: Turn undead as full cleric level (not 2 lower)
Detect Undead at will (30 ft, concentration)
+3 to saves vs. undead special attacks
special_hindrances: Must destroy all undead encountered — cannot parley with them
Seen as grim and unsettling by non-adventuring NPCs (-1 CHA reaction in social situations)

kits.name: Inquisitor
kits.description: A paladin empowered to root out corruption within the faith.
special_benefits: Detect Alignment at will
+2 to saves vs. illusion and deception
Legal authority to question and detain within the church
special_hindrances: Distrusted by all — even allies are held to strict standards
Must report all transgressions to the church — cannot turn a blind eye

kits.name: Militarist
kits.description: A paladin trained as a military officer and battle leader.
special_benefits: +1 to attack rolls for all allies within 20 ft (leadership aura)
Can coordinate flanking attacks with allies without requiring verbal communication
+1 to group initiative rolls when leading
special_hindrances: Must follow a military chain of command — cannot act independently without authorization
Cannot refuse a direct order from a superior officer of their faith

kits.name: Votary
kits.description: A cloistered paladin deeply devoted to mystical aspects of their faith.
special_benefits: Extra 1st-level priest spell per day
Paladin spell access at 8th level instead of 9th
+2 to all saving throws vs. spells
special_hindrances: Must spend 4 hours per day in prayer (or lose spell access that day)
Cannot use wealth for personal comfort — must donate all beyond subsistence

kits.name: Wyrmslayer
kits.description: A paladin devoted to hunting and slaying dragons.
bonus_proficiencies: Dragon Lore (special — identifies dragon types and abilities)
special_benefits: +3 to all attack rolls vs. dragons
+2 to saves vs. dragon breath weapons
Detect Dragon presence within 60 ft (passive)
Immune to dragon fear aura at 5th level
special_hindrances: Must pursue dragons when known to be threatening a region
Compelled to accept any challenge from a dragon who calls out this paladin specifically

kits.name: Blade
kits.description: An acrobatic performer who combines juggling, tumbling, and weaponry for entertainment — and combat.
special_benefits: Can fight and perform simultaneously (no penalty)
+1 to attack rolls with finesse weapons when entertaining
Weapon display: may make an intimidation check at start of combat using performance skill
special_hindrances: Must perform regularly to stay sharp
Overconfident in combat — must WIS check to retreat

kits.name: Herald
kits.description: An official messenger and recorder of history, with diplomatic immunity in most cultures.
special_benefits: Diplomatic immunity in civilized areas (cannot be arrested on sight)
+4 to reaction rolls when acting as an official herald
+2 to all History and Legend Lore checks
special_hindrances: Bound by neutrality — cannot take sides in political disputes while on official business
Carries official documents: must deliver them or face severe social consequences

kits.name: Jester
kits.description: A trickster performer who hides wit and cunning behind foolishness.
special_benefits: +2 to reaction from royalty and nobility (entertainment value)
Can insult enemies: target suffers -2 to attack rolls if they fail a WIS check (distracted by mockery)
Effectively invisible as a threat — enemies rarely target a jester first
special_hindrances: Not taken seriously: -2 to reaction in serious situations (war councils, tribunals)
Cannot resist an opportunity for a prank or joke even in danger

kits.name: Loremaster
kits.description: A scholarly bard devoted to collecting and preserving knowledge.
special_benefits: Legend Lore improves: base 10% + 5% per level
Access to rare libraries: +2 to any knowledge-based check if time is taken to research
Identify magic items: base 15% per level (instead of 5%)
special_hindrances: Must preserve and record knowledge — cannot destroy books or historical items
Compelled to learn: will delay adventures for access to a rare library or informant

kits.name: Meistersinger
kits.description: A disciplined musical virtuoso trained in a formal tradition.
special_benefits: Bardic voice ability affects +2 additional creatures
+2 to counter-song checks
Once per day: perform a song that heals 1d4 HP to all allies who hear it
special_hindrances: Bound by the guild of meistersingers — must pay dues and attend competitions
Will not perform crude or vulgar entertainment — reputation is everything

kits.name: Skald
kits.description: A warrior-bard from a northern tradition who sings of battle and honors the dead.
special_benefits: Can wear chain mail without arcane spell failure
+2 to attack bonus inspiration (instead of the normal +1)
Battle chant: maintain bardic inspiration during combat without concentration check
special_hindrances: Must compose and recite a saga about any heroic deed witnessed or performed
Cannot wear plate armor or heavier
Bound by a strong sense of personal honor

kits.name: Thespian
kits.description: A dramatic actor and performer skilled in playing many roles.
special_benefits: +20% to Disguise ability
Can impersonate specific individuals (+10% with 30 minutes of observation)
+2 to reaction from all NPCs in social situations
special_hindrances: Melodramatic: WIS check required to resist playing up a scene for dramatic effect
Cannot stay in one identity for more than 1 week without becoming confused about true self (WIS check)

druid.notes: Druid kits (called Totemic Druids or Terrain Druids) specialize in a specific environment or spiritual tradition.

kits.name: Forest Druid
kits.description: Deeply bonded to woodlands and their creatures.
special_benefits: Cannot be surprised in a forest
Animal followers include forest creatures only but +2 to number attracted
+2 to spells cast in a forest (range or duration extended by 10%)
special_hindrances: Will not leave forested areas willingly
-2 to all rolls in deserts or arctic terrain

kits.name: Mountain Druid
kits.description: Guardian of high peaks and rocky terrain.
special_benefits: Immune to altitude sickness
Can sense earthquakes and volcanic activity 1d4 hours in advance
+2 to saves vs. elemental earth effects
special_hindrances: Uncomfortable below 2,000 ft elevation for extended periods
Will not allow mining that damages sacred peaks

kits.name: Plains Druid
kits.description: Protector of open grasslands and the herds that live there.
special_benefits: Can communicate with herd animals (horses, bison, cattle) as innate ability
+4 to initiative in open terrain
+2 to tracking on plains
special_hindrances: Claustrophobic: -1 to all rolls underground or in enclosed spaces
Will not damage grassland ecosystems

kits.name: Swamp Druid
kits.description: Master of bogs, marshes, and wetlands.
special_benefits: Immune to diseases native to swamps
Can move through swamp terrain at full speed
+2 to saves vs. poison
special_hindrances: Disturbing appearance in cities: -1 CHA reaction
Must return to a swamp for at least 1 week per month or lose +1 to spell levels temporarily

kits.name: Desert Druid
kits.description: Survivor of arid wastelands, attuned to heat and scarcity.
special_benefits: Needs only 1/4 normal water requirement
Immune to natural heat
Can find water underground within 60 ft (innate)
+2 to camouflage/hide in desert terrain
special_hindrances: Uncomfortable in cold or wet environments: -1 to all rolls in arctic or water-heavy terrain
Will protect desert oases and trade routes from exploitation

kits.name: Sea Druid
kits.description: Servant of ocean deities and guardian of the seas.
special_benefits: Breathe water as naturally as air
Commune with sea creatures (fish, dolphins, whales) as innate ability
+2 to all saves vs. cold, drowning, and electrical attacks
special_hindrances: Loses powers if more than 1 day from a significant body of water
Will not allow ocean dumping or magical pollution of the sea

psionicist.notes: Psionicist kits are based on the mental discipline or school of thought the psionicist follows.

kits.name: The Sensei
kits.description: A wise teacher of mental disciplines who trains others while continuing their own development.
special_benefits: Can teach psionic devotions to others (1 week per devotion)
+2 to all Telepathy rolls
Calm Emotions at will (as a free action, once per day)
special_hindrances: Must take on at least 1 student
Cannot harm a student or surrender one to danger

kits.name: The Marauder
kits.description: A combative psionicist who uses psionic powers aggressively and offensively.
special_benefits: +1 PSP regeneration per hour of combat
+2 to attack rolls with psionic combat modes
Can enter psionic combat as a free action (no initiative loss)
special_hindrances: Must win psionic challenges when issued (pride)
Viewed with distrust by more spiritual psionicists

kits.name: The Nomad
kits.description: A psychoportation specialist who wanders the planes.
special_benefits: All psychoportation power costs reduced by 1 PSP (minimum 1)
Can sense planar boundaries and dimensional rifts within 30 ft
+2 to saves vs. teleportation mishaps
special_hindrances: Drawn to dimensional anomalies — compelled to investigate
Cannot stay in the same plane for more than 1 month voluntarily
